one_line_description: Close is a sales-focused CRM with built-in calling, email, and SMS for inside sales teams.
auth_summary: Close supports API keys ('for scripts') created self-serve under Settings > Developer > API Keys, and OAuth 2.0 for user-facing integrations (including OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration on its MCP server). The MCP server also accepts a Close-API-Key header with a Close-Scope header (mcp.read / mcp.write_safe / mcp.write_destructive).
access_notes: API keys are self-serve from account settings (Settings > Developer > API Keys), but Close is a paid product with a free trial and no permanent free tier; docs mention creating OAuth apps and getting listed, and direct some partner/API inquiries to support@close.com.
api_surface_summary: REST API covering leads, contacts, opportunities, activities (calls/emails/SMS), custom fields/objects, sequences, bulk email/SMS actions, webhooks, event logs, and reporting metrics.
agent_notes: Official remote MCP server at https://mcp.close.com/mcp (HTTP Streamable, OAuth 2.0 DCR, officially supported in the Claude ecosystem) with tiered write scopes - a strong signal for agent-readiness. One gap: docs I fetched did not state the exact wire format for API keys on the core REST API (widely known to be HTTP Basic auth with the key as username, but I could not confirm from an official page I saw, so Basic auth is not listed as a distinct method). Plan-level API access restrictions were not specified in fetched docs.
